mainmatter.p: Let A be a pointed dcpo with an open positivity predicate; we define the “purification” map { A } \xrightarrow {{ \mathsf { purify } _A }}{ \mathsf { L } {A^+} } into the lift of the subdcpo of positive elements to send a \in A to the partial element { \mathopen {} \left ( \mathsf { pos } \, {a}, \lambda p. a \right ) \mathclose {}}; we note:
mainmatter.tex: \mathsf { purify } _Aa = { \mathopen {} \left ( \mathsf { pos } \, {a}, \lambda p. a \right ) \mathclose {}} = \bigsqcup _{p: \mathsf { pos } \, a} \eta _Aa
mainmatter.p: The mapping defined above is continuous because we have assumed that A has an open positivity predicate, so its domain of definition defines a Scott-open subdcpo of A.

frontmatter.title: Characterizing freeness in terms of positivity

mainmatter.p: In classical mathematics, every \mathbb {L}-algebra is free; this does not appear to be asserted by constructive mathematics, but in this section we verify that freeness can be characterized in terms of positivity. The main result is that a pointed dcpo A is positively generated if and only the evident map { \mathsf { L } {A^+} } \xrightarrow {{ }}{ A } is an isomorphism.

frontmatter.title: Pointed dcpos with open positivity predicates are almost free

mainmatter.p: Let A be a pointed dcpo with an open positivity predicate; then the purification map { A } \xrightarrow {{ \mathsf { purify } _A }}{ \mathsf { L } {A^+} } is the projection part of an embedding-projection pair { \mathopen {} \left ( \mathsf { L } { \iota ^+}; \alpha _A \right ) \mathclose {}} \dashv \mathsf { purify } _A.

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: We fix { \mathopen {} \left ( \phi ,a^+ \right ) \mathclose {}} : \mathsf { L } {A^+} and compute explicitly using the fact that \mathsf { purify } _A preserves the bottom element and therefore preserves semidirected subsets:

For the deflation, we fix a:A and observe that
\alpha _A { \mathopen {} \left ( \mathsf { L } { \iota ^+} { \mathopen {} \left ( \mathsf { purify } _A a \right ) \mathclose {}} \right ) \mathclose {}} = \bigsqcup _{p: \mathsf { pos } \, a} a which is evidently below a.

frontmatter.title: Positively generated pointed dcpos are free

mainmatter.p: Let A be a positively generated pointed dcpo; then A is canonically free on its subdcpo of positive elements A^+. More specifically, the embedding-projection pair { \mathopen {} \left ( \mathsf { L } { \iota ^+}; \alpha _A \right ) \mathclose {}} \dashv { A } \xrightarrow {{ \mathsf { purify } _A }}{ \mathsf { L } {A^+} } given by the purification map is an isomorphism of dcpos.

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: First, we recall that the embedding-projection pair { \mathopen {} \left ( \mathsf { L } { \iota ^+}; \alpha _A \right ) \mathclose {}} \dashv \mathsf { purify } _A exists because A has an open positivity predicate by virtue of its positive generation. Therefore, the statement of this lemma is well-formed.

Because { \mathopen {} \left ( \mathsf { L } { \iota ^+}; \alpha _A \right ) \mathclose {}} \dashv \mathsf { purify } _A is an embedding-projection pair, it remains only to check that \mathsf { purify } _A; \mathsf { L } { \iota ^+}; \alpha _A is an inflation, i.e. for all a:A we must have a \sqsubseteq \alpha _A { \mathopen {} \left ( \mathsf { L } { \iota ^+} { \mathopen {} \left ( \mathsf { purify } _Aa \right ) \mathclose {}} \right ) \mathclose {}}. As the latter is equal to \bigsqcup _{p: \mathsf { pos } \, a}a, the inflation condition follows from our assumption that A is positively generated.

mainmatter.p: A pointed dcpo A is lifted if and only if it is positively generated.

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: This follows from two existing lemmas:

ol.li: Lifted dcpos (free algebras) are positively generated.
Positively generated pointed dcpos are free.

mainmatter.p: We say that a pointed dcpo A has an open positivity predicate if and only if any of the following equivalent conditions hold:

ol.li: The positivity predicate { \Gamma A } \xrightarrow {{ \mathsf { pos } }}{ \Omega } tracks a continuous morphism of dcpos { A } \xrightarrow {{ \mathsf { pos } }}{ \Sigma }.
The embedding { A^+ } \xrightarrow {{ \iota ^+ }}{ A } is a Scott-open immersion.
For a directed subset I \subseteq A, if \bigsqcup {A} is positive then there exists an element a \in I that is positive in A.

mainmatter.p: A dcpo is called pointed when it has a bottom element \bot under the information order.

frontmatter.title: The subdcpo spanned by positive elements

mainmatter.p: Let A be a pointed dcpo; then the subposet A^+ \subseteq A spanned by positive is a dcpo.

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: This follows from the closure of positive elements in an algebra under directed suprema.

frontmatter.title: Positively generated pointed dcpos are free

mainmatter.p: Let A be a positively generated pointed dcpo; then A is canonically free on its subdcpo of positive elements A^+. More specifically, the embedding-projection pair { \mathopen {} \left ( \mathsf { L } { \iota ^+}; \alpha _A \right ) \mathclose {}} \dashv { A } \xrightarrow {{ \mathsf { purify } _A }}{ \mathsf { L } {A^+} } given by the purification map is an isomorphism of dcpos.

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: First, we recall that the embedding-projection pair { \mathopen {} \left ( \mathsf { L } { \iota ^+}; \alpha _A \right ) \mathclose {}} \dashv \mathsf { purify } _A exists because A has an open positivity predicate by virtue of its positive generation. Therefore, the statement of this lemma is well-formed.

Because { \mathopen {} \left ( \mathsf { L } { \iota ^+}; \alpha _A \right ) \mathclose {}} \dashv \mathsf { purify } _A is an embedding-projection pair, it remains only to check that \mathsf { purify } _A; \mathsf { L } { \iota ^+}; \alpha _A is an inflation, i.e. for all a:A we must have a \sqsubseteq \alpha _A { \mathopen {} \left ( \mathsf { L } { \iota ^+} { \mathopen {} \left ( \mathsf { purify } _Aa \right ) \mathclose {}} \right ) \mathclose {}}. As the latter is equal to \bigsqcup _{p: \mathsf { pos } \, a}a, the inflation condition follows from our assumption that A is positively generated.

frontmatter.title: Pointed dcpos with open positivity predicates are almost free

mainmatter.p: Let A be a pointed dcpo with an open positivity predicate; then the purification map { A } \xrightarrow {{ \mathsf { purify } _A }}{ \mathsf { L } {A^+} } is the projection part of an embedding-projection pair { \mathopen {} \left ( \mathsf { L } { \iota ^+}; \alpha _A \right ) \mathclose {}} \dashv \mathsf { purify } _A.

block.headline: Proof.
block.p: We fix { \mathopen {} \left ( \phi ,a^+ \right ) \mathclose {}} : \mathsf { L } {A^+} and compute explicitly using the fact that \mathsf { purify } _A preserves the bottom element and therefore preserves semidirected subsets:

For the deflation, we fix a:A and observe that
\alpha _A { \mathopen {} \left ( \mathsf { L } { \iota ^+} { \mathopen {} \left ( \mathsf { purify } _A a \right ) \mathclose {}} \right ) \mathclose {}} = \bigsqcup _{p: \mathsf { pos } \, a} a which is evidently below a.
